finding: May be associated with: musculoskeletal scleroderma

differentials.name: edema of distal portion of extremities
differentials.raw: edema of distal portion of extremities

differentials.name: thickened inelastic waxy skin most prominent about face + extremities, symmetrical polyarthralgias
differentials.raw: thickened inelastic waxy skin most prominent about face + extremities, symmetrical polyarthralgias (50-80%)

differentials.name: Raynaud phenomenon
differentials.raw: Raynaud phenomenon (may precede other symptoms by months / years)

differentials.name: atrophy + thickening of skin and musculature (78%) @   Fingers
differentials.raw: atrophy + thickening of skin and musculature (78%) @   Fingers

differentials.name: "sausage digit" = edema of digits associated with loss of transverse skin folds + lack of definition of subcutaneous fat
differentials.raw: "sausage digit" = edema of digits associated with loss of transverse skin folds + lack of definition of subcutaneous fat

differentials.name: stiffness in small joints, occasionally in knee, shoulder, wrist; lack of motility → eventually contractures
differentials.raw: stiffness in small joints, occasionally in knee, shoulder, wrist; lack of motility → eventually contractures

differentials.name: slightly productive, mostly dry cough, atypical chest pain
differentials.raw: slightly productive, mostly dry cough, atypical chest pain

differentials.name: pulmonary function abnormalities in the absence of frank roentgenographic changes ( TYPICAL

context: Musculoskeletal Scleroderma √  "tapered fingers" = sclerodactyly = atrophy + resorption of soft tissues of fingertips + soft-tissue calcifications √  acroosteolysis (63-80%) =  "penciling" / "autoamputation" = resorption of distal phalanges of hand beginning at volar aspect of terminal tufts with proximal progression @   Subcutaneous calcinosis (25-58%) Location:   extensor surface, areas with pressure / friction √  calcinosis = punctate soft-tissue calcifications of fingertips, axilla, ischial tuberosity, forearm, elbow (over pressure area), lower leg, face √  calcifications around tendons, b
